title: Buttery Light Ethiopian Turkey Neck Stew
description: A fragrant, buttery onepot stew featuring tender turkey necks, berbere spice, and fresh vegetables for a light yet satisfying Ethiopian-inspired dinner.
instructions: Rinse turkey necks and pat dry.
Heat butter in a large pot over medium heat until foamy; brown turkey necks on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove necks and set aside.
Add diced onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger to the pot; sauté until onion is translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in berbere spice mix and tomato paste; cook for 1 minute to bloom the flavors.
Return turkey necks to the pot and pour in chicken broth. Add sliced carrots and potatoes; stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook for 55–60 minutes, or until turkey necks are tender and reach an internal temperature of 71 °C (160 °F).
Taste and season with salt and pepper as needed.
Stir in chopped cilantro just before serving; adjust seasoning if desired.
Serve hot with a side of injera, millet bread, or steamed rice.
Enjoy the buttery, aromatic flavors of this Ethiopian turkey neck stew!
